elements.sent-2411-005: The paper studies a regularization of the quantum (effective) action for a scalar field theory in a general position on a compact smooth Riemannian manifold.
elements.sent-2411-006: As the main method, we propose the use of a special averaging operator, which leads to a quasi-locality and is a natural generalization of a cutoff regularization in the coordinate representation in the case of a curved metric.
elements.sent-2411-007: It is proved that the regularization method is consistent with a process of gluing of manifolds and partition functions, that is, with the transition from submanifolds to the main manifold using an additional functional integration.
elements.sent-2411-008: It is shown that the method extends to other models, and is also consistent with the process of multiplicative renormalization.
elements.sent-2411-009: Additionally, we discuss issues related to the correct introduction of regularization and the locality.
elements.sent-2411-010: Key words and phrases: effective action, quantum action, partition function, deformation, cutoff regularization, averaging operator, gluing of manifolds, gluing of partition functions, functional integral, scalar theory, quasi-locality, renormalization.
elements.sent-2411-011: Currently, the functional integration method \cite{sk-1,sk-2,3 is a widely used tool for studying quantum field models \cite{9,10 and, as a rule, implies a transition to an formal series expansion with respect to some auxiliary small parameter.}}
elements.sent-2411-012: Thus, in a suitable limit, the calculation of the "functional integral" is reduced to calculating the coefficients of the series, which can be represented in the form of well-studied Gaussian integrals.
elements.sent-2411-013: The use of such techniques is more of a necessity, since a complete mathematical description for the "functional integral" does not yet exist.
elements.sent-2411-014: Nevertheless, the method occupies a key position in modern mathematical and theoretical physics.
elements.sent-2411-015: One of the problems associated with the study of the mentioned series is the presence of divergences, see \cite{105 .}
elements.sent-2411-016: This is due to the fact that the coefficients of the series are constructed using Green's functions (fundamental solutions of a special kind, see \cite{Gelfand-1964,Vladimirov-2002 ), nonlinear combinations of which can lead either to the appearance of non-integrable densities or to singular functionals at singular points.}
elements.sent-2411-017: Regularization and elimination (renormalization) of such objects play an important role \cite{6,7 , since they allow not only to establish new mathematical relations or understand the internal structure of the coefficients, but also to give a clear physical meaning to the quantities under consideration.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Recently, the field of mathematics related to the functorial quantum field theory \cite{sk-3,sk-4,sk-5,sk-6,sk-7 has been actively developing.}
elements.sent-2411-019: Despite the fact that at the moment there are many studied models \cite{sk-8,sk-9,sk-10 , examples related to the consideration of well-known quantum field densities on Riemannian manifolds are quite rare \cite{sk-11,sk-12,sk-4,sk-15,sk-13,sk-14,sk-16 .}}
elements.sent-2411-020: One of the main problems in this area is the task of regularization and elimination of divergences.
elements.sent-2411-021: In particular, it is unclear how to construct a regularization that would work in all dimensions and be consistent with the process of "gluing" of partition functions, that is, with the transition from two smooth Riemannian submanifolds to a third smooth Riemannian manifold by connecting along a common boundary.
elements.sent-2411-022: This paper describes the construction of a universal quasi-local regularization based on a deformation of a classical action by adding a deforming operator to the term responsible for the interaction.
elements.sent-2411-023: It is shown that the proposed regularization works in all dimensions and is consistent with the process of gluing manifolds and partition functions.
elements.sent-2411-024: It is important to note that in this paper the described procedure is a generalization of a cutoff regularization in the coordinate representation for the case of a smooth Riemannian manifold with a boundary.
elements.sent-2411-025: This approach was first proposed in \cite{34 and then significantly improved in subsequent works \cite{Iv-2024-1,Ivanov-Akac,Ivanov-Kharuk-2020,Ivanov-Kharuk-20222,Ivanov-Kharuk-2023,Iv-Kh-2024,Kh-2024 .}}
elements.sent-2411-026: The peculiarity of this approach is the transparency of the spectral meaning, the connection with the averaging operator \cite{Ivanov-2022,Iv-2024 , the fulfillment of an "applicability condition", see also\footnote{The paper \cite{sk-b-20 discusses the possibility of presenting the answer for averages from \cite{Iv-2024 in elementary functions.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-027: At the same time, it is important to pay attention to the fact that other approaches to regularization are used when working with standard quantum field models in flat space.
elements.sent-2411-028: Among the most popular types, an alternative version of a cutoff regularization can be distinguished \cite{w6,w7,w8,Khar-2020,sk-b-19 , implicit regularization \cite{chi-0,chi-1,chi-2 , Pauli--Villars regularization \cite{Pauli-Villars , and also dimensional one \cite{19,555 and using higher covariant derivatives \cite{Bakeyev-Slavnov,29-st .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: At the same time, the last two play an essential role in theoretical physics and are currently indispensable from the point of view of multi-loop calculations.
elements.sent-2411-030: However, all these regularizations are poorly compatible with the use of classical mathematical physics, and consistency with the process of gluing manifolds and partition functions is either absent or unclear.
elements.sent-2411-031: Moreover, the approach to regularization proposed in this paper seems to be distinctive, since it not only has a strict mathematical formulation that preserves the applicability of classical mathematical physics, but also includes a large number of explicit loop calculations already performed within the framework of standard quantum field models.
elements.sent-2411-032: In particular, consistency has been demonstrated in various theories with the results obtained using other regularization methods widely used in physics and having problems on Riemannian manifolds.
elements.sent-2411-033: Thus, it can be argued that the proposed approach is consistent not only with the process of gluing of manifolds, but also, paying attention to the conceptual component, is consistent with the gluing of two fields of science, mathematical and theoretical physics, in the context of quantum field theory.

elements.sent-2411-038: In the last section, we introduce a quantum (effective) action in the form of an formal series in powers of a small parameter $\sqrt{\hbar}$ and in the form of a formal "functional integral".
elements.sent-2411-039: Section \ref{sk:sec:reg contains a description of the process of regularization of the quantum action and a partition function by deformation of the classical action.}
elements.sent-2411-040: The first part of the section is devoted to the description of the already well-known method of a cutoff regularization in coordinate representation in Euclidean space.
elements.sent-2411-041: Next, the approach is generalized to the case of a smooth Riemannian manifold with a boundary.
elements.sent-2411-042: In the last part, we formulate the main recipe for the introduction of the regularization and also discuss a quasi-locality.

elements.sent-2411-049: In Corollary \ref{sk-c-10 , the possibility of an operator structure of the coupling coefficients is discussed.}
elements.sent-2411-050: In Corollary \ref{sk-c-11 , we discuss the issue of consistency with the multiplicative renormalization process.}

elements.sent-2411-245: However, a natural question arises: "At what stage should the deformation be introduced?".
elements.sent-2411-246: Our answer, based primarily on a large number of calculations in standard models from quantum field theory, is that the deformation should be introduced at the stage of determining the classical action.
elements.sent-2411-247: Various arguments can be given here, including the feasibility of the connection beetwen quantum equations of motion and quantum actions before and after the deformation, but we pay attention to only one specific reason.
elements.sent-2411-248: Although it is less formalized, at the same time it is strategic in nature.
elements.sent-2411-249: As it is known, the functional integral does not currently have a strict mathematical description, except, of course, in some very simple cases.
elements.sent-2411-250: For this reason, when studying quantum field models, one has to work with perturbative decompositions, for which nothing else is required except the "normalization" and the ability to calculate standard Gaussian integrals.
elements.sent-2411-251: Given this, a general view of solving direct and inverse problems in mathematical physics can be extended to the functional integral method.

elements.sent-2411-414: Historical background to the title page.
elements.sent-2411-415: Nikolai Maksimovich Kalita was born on December 19, 1925 in the village of Karkhovka, Chernihiv Oblast of the Ukrainian SSR in a peasant family.
elements.sent-2411-416: His parents were natives of the same village Maxim Titovich Kalita, who was born on January 21, 1899 and died during the Great Patriotic War at the front, and Domna Vasilyevna Kalita (Kostenko), who was born on October 10, 1900 and met old age in Girmanka.
elements.sent-2411-417: Their lineage can be traced back to the metrical books relating to the Ioanno-Bogoslovskaya Church.
elements.sent-2411-418: According to the entries in N.M.'s work book, he served in the Soviet Army from December 11, 1944 to October 13, 1978 and retired with the rank of major.
elements.sent-2411-419: He married Lydia Matveevna Mukhopad on September 2, 1951 in Lviv, who was born on March 18 (April 17, according to documents), 1929 in the khutor of Babakovka in the Bilopillia Raion of the Sumy Oblast.
elements.sent-2411-420: Later, they had two children: Svetlana (28.01.1953, Horodok, Lviv Oblast) and Natalia (06.05.1956, Lutsk, Volyn Oblast).
elements.sent-2411-421: After retiring in the 80s, he and his family moved to Donetsk, where he lived until his death on December 8, 2000.. His wife L.M.
